item.title: Scooter crash leaves Central Coast mum fighting for life in Bali hospital
item.description: A mother of two from the NSW Central Coast was holidaying in Bali with her girlfriends when she was hit by a truck while riding a scooter.

item.title: Saltwater crocodile found in dam hundreds of kilometres from normal habitat
item.description: Authorities say they will rehome a 2.3 metre saltwater crocodile that was found in a dam near Gatton, hundreds of kilometres from its usual habitat.

item.title: Farmers stockpile diesel as Middle East conflict prompts fuel supply fears
item.description: Farmers say consumers could soon feel the impact of fuel shortages and soaring prices at the checkout, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: As the Iran war rages on, historic oil price surge sends share markets into a spin
item.description: For more than a year, traders have been pushing markets higher on the hopes of an AI-powered economic miracle and the notion of TACO — Trump Always Chickens Out. Now it looks like the penny has dropped.

item.title: Big Red Bash site will not be dry enough to hold festival in July, search for new venue
item.description: The event is still four months away, but organisers say the site will not be sufficiently dry in time.

item.title: Katherine locals survey damage from major flooding as volunteer 'army' rallies to help
item.description: An "army" of volunteers has hit the streets of Katherine to help residents worst affected by the weekend's flooding, as they survey the damage from near-record river rises.

item.title: Labor MP warns against 'dangerous' linking of Israeli government to antisemitism
item.description: Josh Burns also acknowledged the government had not always acted fast enough to deal with antisemitism and said it was necessary to be "humble" in the wake of the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Farmers, community groups call for wild deer to be labelled a pest as population balloons
item.description: The Tasmanian government asked the public what it thought about how wild fallow deer were being managed. It received hundreds of submissions calling for deer to be labelled a pest and for improved zoning and hunting permits.

item.title: The rise of cowboy core: What's behind it and what does it mean for Australian brands?
item.description: From streetwear to festival fits, country fashion has become mainstream in Australian cities and heritage brands are reaping the benefits.

item.title: The Bride! Is a mismatched metatextual movie marriage
item.description: The Bride!, a modern retelling of The Bride of Frankenstein, takes massive swings in terms of performances, plotting and subtext, but not all of its gambles pay off.

item.title: Higher oil and fuel prices hit road transport industry that moves 'everything you buy in shops'
item.description: Increasing fuel prices are putting pressure on the road transport industry, with the potential to impact the price of everything from furniture to food.

item.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz: How well doest thou know his characters and quotes? Testeth thineself hither
item.description: A quiz by any other word would smell as sweet, or something, so have a crack at our 10 questions on the works of William Shakespeare.

item.title: Australian Border Force intercepts 150,000 units of melatonin amid crackdown on unregulated products
item.description: Australian Border Force officials have intercepted more than 150,000 units of melatonin in four months, after authorities warned they would crack down on unregulated products.

item.title: Brisbane Lions to fight Harris Andrews and Darcy Gardiner bans at AFL tribunal
item.description: Brisbane begins its season with two pivotal cases at the AFL tribunal in a bid to not have its defensive stocks completely depleted heading into the first month.

item.title: How the 'tough guy' of South-East Asia is reshaping Australia's largest neighbour
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's big ambitions on the world stage and at home have alarmed his critics.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn quarterfinal opponent as North Korea sensationally protests offside call
item.description: The Matildas will face North Korea in the quarterfinals of the Women's Asian Cup, after the North Koreans go down 2-1 to China in controversial circumstances at a soaking Western Sydney Stadium.

item.title: Current and former Liberals are quietly behind two 'grassroots' childcare campaigns
item.description: The childcare groups say they are the voices for everyday parents. What they do not mention is that they are supported by a network of current and former Liberals.

item.title: Decline in GP home visits drives vulnerable people into care homes
item.description: The number of GP home visits has declined by 85 per cent in Australia over the past three decades, which doctors say is driving vulnerable people into residential care and hospital prematurely.

item.title: Scientists call for urgent funding as hundreds of CSIRO job cuts loom
item.description: Scientists are calling on the Australian government to push back on a plan to scrap hundreds of jobs at the nation's science agency, as they await the release of further details this week.

item.title: Michael Milton overcome with emotion after ending two-decade hiatus by competing at 2026 Paralympics
item.description: Michael Milton is overcome with emotion after making his Paralympics return, but is less than impressed by his actual performance on the slopes.

item.title: Patient information should not be used as 'political tool', peak doctors body says
item.description: The Australian Medical Association says politicians should not be using non-consensual patient information to make a point about the health system.

item.title: Widower of roadworks traffic controller still seeking answers 13 years after truck killed wife
item.description: Colin Caudell has been on a mission to improve safety for roadside workers since his wife Suzanne was struck and killed by a truck in 2013.

item.title: Sixth case of flesh-eating Buruli ulcer appears on NSW South Coast
item.description: The sixth case of a flesh-eating Buruli ulcer has been detected on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Three dead in southern NSW after head-on crash on Kings Highway at Monga
item.description: Three people are dead and four others injured after a shocking head-on crash, after car crossed onto wrong side of Kings Highway in southern NSW.

item.title: Residents in four Bundaberg suburbs urged to 'leave immediately' amid major flood warning for Burnett River
item.description: Residents in four Bundaberg suburbs have been issued with an emergency warning to "leave immediately" ahead of major flooding on the Burnett River, which runs through the city.

item.title: Sharing of wrong patient's email provides rare public glimpse into the cunning world of political spin
item.description: It is common for media advisors to provide reporters with background information in an effort to shape the way a story is reported, but the practice backfired spectacularly when the SA government shared an email from the wrong patient.

item.title: David Littleproud steps down as Nationals leader, coy on who should replace him — as it happened
item.description: David Littleproud announces he is stepping down as Nationals leader, saying he believes it is time to transition to a new leader.

item.title: Woman shares her story of loss to encourage road users to stay safe
item.description: Lily Mathews's father died while drink driving four years ago. She hopes others can learn from her trauma.

item.title: ASX closes 1pc higher, petrol prices up 17c a litre in last week — as it happened
item.description: The Australian market closed 1 per cent higher, regaining some of yesterday's losses. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Australia to provide military support to Gulf states attacked by Iran
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirms the Australian Defence Force will deploy an E-7A Wedgetail aircraft to the Gulf to help protect and defend the region, providing long-range reconnaissance activity.

item.title: Jungle King boat on Saxon Reef
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rainforest river cruise boat found on Great Barrier Reef after flash flood
item.description: Operators in the world heritage Daintree Rainforest are anxious about lost business after tour boats and ferries were damaged in floods ahead of the Easter holidays.

item.title: Residents raise concerns about housing development after unprecedented floods
item.description: Residents of a Central Victorian town are frustrated after being hit with two unprecedented flooding events in a couple of days.

item.title: Boil water alert issued, tap pressure to be reduced amid 'unprecedented' flooding at Darwin's main drinking supply
item.description: A boil water alert has been issued and tap pressure will be reduced as water authorities grapple with "unprecedented" flooding at Darwin River Dam.

item.title: Slaying the AI dragon and appealing for the human touch in performance in Perth Festival debut
item.description: Never mind artificial intelligence, this is dragon intelligence. A new play commissioned for the Perth Festival explores the importance of human creativity, even if AI can help with an unfinished novel.

item.title: Sheep stuck in flood waters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rain blankets properties in far west NSW
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Far West NSW graziers who went from drought to flooding rains begin clean-up and search for stock
item.description: As water begins to recede after record-breaking rains, outback New South Wales graziers are assessing the damage and searching for stock.

item.title: Labor to pass super tax changes with support of Greens
item.description: The legislation comes as speculation builds that the federal government will pursue more ambitious tax changes in the May budget.

item.title: Bundaberg residents told to 'leave immediately' as floodwaters rise, bridges to shut — as it happened
item.description: A major flood is threatening Bundaberg after days of rain spilled from dams and set rivers on the rise across Queensland.

item.title: Australian women's cricket captain Sophie Molineux returns for tour of West Indies
item.description: Australia's women's cricket team receives a boost for the West Indies series, with Sophie Molineux expected to return from her back injury.

item.title: Hungary seizes money and gold in deepening diplomatic spat with Ukraine
item.description: Hungary has seized around $115 million and gold from a Ukrainian bank transfer and temporarily detained seven Ukrainians, as Budapest pressures Kyiv to restart oil shipments from Russia.

item.title: US and Arab states turn to Ukraine for help against Iranian drone attacks
item.description: Ukraine is called upon to provide drone defence expertise to the US and Gulf states as the region struggles to counter swarms of Iranian attack drones.

item.title: Iranian soccer players escape minders and secure asylum in Australia
item.description: Australian officials waited on the sidelines of the Iranian football team's final game in the Gold Coast on Sunday, signalling to players help was available if they wanted to talk. The government has granted asylum to five athletes who escaped from their hotel with assistance from Australian Federal Police.

item.title: Why Alex Johnston's pursuit of the all-time try-scoring record is a wonder half a century in the making
item.description: As Alex Johnston moves to the edge of forever in his chase of the all-time try-scoring record, rugby league must brace itself for a chance that only comes around once or twice a century.

item.title: Middle East conflict hiking fertiliser costs for Australian farmers but what does it mean for grocery prices?
item.description: The Middle East conflict is driving up the cost of an essential fertiliser for Australian farmers, but there are other factors more likely to affect grocery prices.

item.title: Five things I do (as a therapist) when anxiety takes over
item.description: No-one is harder on me than me. Anxiety thrives on this. Along the way, I've built a toolkit that helps me regulate, reconnect and come back to myself.

item.title: Heavy rain continues for northern Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Who are the Iranian Women's Asian Cup players granted asylum by Australia?
item.description: Australia has granted five Iranian players asylum after their Women's Asian Cup campaign. So, who are they?

item.title: Heavy rainfall continues to threaten Katherine's flood recovery as rivers swell in Beswick, Daly River
item.description: More rain could see river levels rise even further, threatening remote NT communities, while the clean-up has started in Katherine and businesses reopen.

item.title: Push for investigation into safety, durability of Great Western Highway's historic Convict Bridge
item.description: The Blue Mountains community warned the NSW government the Great Western Highway's historic Convict Bridge could not withstand modern day traffic prior to its closure last week.

item.title: What is a Wedgetail? And why is Australia sending missiles to the Iran conflict?
item.description: One of Australia's most sophisticated military surveillance planes will be deployed to the Middle East, along with a crew of about 85 defence personnel to operate it.

item.title: Indigenous artworks taken on 5,000km road trip from the Pilbara to Biennale of Sydney
item.description: From the Pilbara to Penrith, an Indigenous WA community has taken its prized cultural and artistic possessions across the country for the Biennale of Sydney.

item.title: Darwin issued a boil water alert, here's what you need to know
item.description: A boil water alert has been issued for Darwin, in addition to Katherine, Beswick and Tindal. The alert comes after issues with the pump station at Darwin's main dam. Here's what you need to know about your water.

item.title: New laws to temporarily ban people coming to Australia from designated countries
item.description: Visitors from some countries could be temporarily blocked from Australia by a federal government concerned about a wave of people overstaying their visas due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands insists he is not in breach of $100m contract over on-air fight with co-host Jackie O
item.description: In his first statement since he was ordered off the air after a blow-up with co-host Jackie "O" Henderson, Sandilands calls on ARN Media, owner of KIIS FM, to "honour our agreement" and let him return to the Kyle and Jackie O Show.

item.title: Zac Lomax admits he is a rugby union 'novice', but has no regrets about end to NRL career
item.description: Rugby recruit Zac Lomax knows he has a long way to go to fulfil his dream of becoming a dual international and playing for the Wallabies at the 2027 World Cup.

item.title: Petrol prices surge across Asia, prompting rationing and price caps
item.description: From closing schools to daily limits, the surging cost of oil prompts countries across Asia to take action to ensure fuel supply and keep prices in check.

item.title: Emotional David Littleproud steps down as leader of the National Party
item.description: The Queenslander cried as he spoke to reporters, saying it had been a "pretty rough" time since the May election.

item.title: How a war in the Middle East caused Australia's sudden fuel price hikes
item.description: If you've topped up on fuel this week, you likely would have noticed prices at the bowser have jumped a fair bit. Here's a quick breakdown on why that happened.

item.title: The government is not speaking plainly about joining the war effort in the Middle East
item.description: It's a reasonable question for Australians to ask given the government is sending a sophisticated reconnaissance plane — crewed by dozens of military personnel — to the Middle East. And legal experts say the answer is yes.

item.title: Migration agent shares details of secret operation to get Iranian football asylum seekers alone
item.description: After the increasing urgency over the fate of Iran's women's football team over the past few days, the breakthrough came last night, when five players were able to seek independent advice.

item.title: Bundaberg residents evacuate homes as major flooding nears its peak
item.description: Bundaberg residents are evacuating their homes and businesses as the city faces its worst flood in more than a decade.

item.title: Lachlan Bowles was wearing Nazi armband during Kellerberrin shooting, inquest hears
item.description: A man who fatally shot his colleague at a Wheatbelt grain silo before turning the weapon on himself had undiagnosed mental health issues and was exploring extremist ideology, an inquest hears.

item.title: Public health patient demands apology after private health info given to media
item.description: Monica Wohlstadt is the second person in a matter of days to raise concerns about how the SA health minister's office used personal patient information to "background" journalists ahead of the publication of stories.

item.title: How Donald Trump's latest Iran war update fits into the US administration's shifting statements
item.description: US President Donald Trump has said the war in Iran could be over "very soon", but has still not offered a definitive end date for the conflict.

item.title: Electoral review proposes scrapping two Queensland seats, changing boundaries of dozens more
item.description: The first review into Queensland's electoral boundaries since 2017 has recommended for two electorates to be abolished, two more created, and almost 50 changed.

item.title: Petrol prices leap to national average of 198 cents per litre, new data shows
item.description: New data has confirmed a significant rise in fuel costs, with the national average petrol price increasing 17 cents over the week to Sunday, to 198 cents a litre, as analysts warn there's more room for it to rise.

item.title: Super Netball's Kiwi influx leaves sour taste for diehard Australian fans
item.description: Nine Kiwi players will feature in Super Netball for 2026, after the New Zealand governing body relaxed its national selection policy. But just like the green fruit, their influx has left a sour taste in the mouth of rusted-on Australian Diamonds fans.

item.title: Fitzroy Crossing's top cop wrangles Kimberley crocodile in backyard
item.description: Fitzroy Crossing police officer-in-charge Mark Howes says no-one else was available to help remove a freshwater crocodile from a local backyard, "so I thought I'd have a crack".

item.title: WA Police officer-in-charge captures crocodile from Kimberley backyard.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hobart's Macquarie Point stadium not expected to host AFL games until 2031
item.description: When the $1.13 billion stadium was approved last year, it was hoped the Tasmania Devils would begin playing AFL games there from 2029. The head of the corporation tasked with delivering the stadium says her expectations are set a little bit later.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Search for missing Port Pirie woman extends to Brighton
item.description: South Australian police expand their search for "high-risk missing person" Patricia Ewens, who has not been seen for almost three weeks.

item.title: Indonesia: Prabowo Power
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US releases footage of missile launch during Iran conflict
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Five Iranian women’s soccer players granted humanitarian visas
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Australia joins the war effort in the Middle East.
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Allegations of discrimination against First Nations staff in ACT Public Service referred to independent watchdog
item.description: Allegations of systemic discrimination against First Nations staff in the ACT Public Service have been referred to the ACT Public Sector Standards Commissioner, amid calls from Indigenous leaders for a formal investigation.

item.title: Is there a crisis in our mental health system?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Seven members of Iran's women's football team have sought asylum, ABC understands
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, who was at the airport ahead of the team's departure, says border force officials made multiple representations to the players before they were due to depart Sydney.

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 for spam email breaches
item.description: The Australian Communications and Media Authority says activewear fashion brand Lululemon sent more than 370,000 emails containing marketing content without an unsubscribe option.

item.title: Kurt Cobain's Smells Like Teen Spirit guitar in auction featuring The Beatles, Prince and Elton John
item.description: The iconic six-string that gave a sound to an era's angst is among a treasure trove of items set to be sold at a New York auction.

item.title: Two new novels, The Heir Apparent and Royal Spin, offer a glimpse behind the curtain of royal life
item.description: With Queen Mary of Denmark soon to arrive in Australia and scandal engulfing the House of Windsor, royals are swamping our news cycle. So why are readers so fascinated by royal fiction?

item.title: Live: Oil prices surge after ships attacked in the Strait of Hormuz — as it happened
item.description: The price of global benchmark Brent crude has surged 5 per cent and European share markets are also down sharply after several major incidents were reported in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Truwana/Cape Barren Island trail camera survey captures blond echidnas, white-footed dunnart and more
item.description: Wildlife cameras snap rare pictures of blond echidnas, a tiny white-footed dunnart, and threatened seabirds on a small island off Tasmania's north coast.

item.title: AFL tribunal upholds Harris Andrews, Darcy Gardiner bans in major blow to Brisbane's defensive stocks
item.description: Brisbane must tackle a red-hot Sydney side without two of its most important defenders after the AFL tribunal upheld bans handed to Harris Andrews and Darcy Gardiner.

item.title: Donald Trump warns Iran against laying mines in Strait of Hormuz as US–Israel strikes intensify
item.description: Donald Trump warns Iran against laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz after US media reports explosives have been placed in the vital shipping lane.

item.title: NSW public health system experienced unprecedented demand, BHI report shows
item.description: The latest figures released by the Bureau of Health Information paints a picture of a health system which remains under immense pressure with record numbers also recorded in admitted patient episodes.

item.title: Hawthorn's Dylan Moore and Connor Macdonald spent night in US jail during off-season
item.description: Dylan Moore opens up on an off-season incident involving a scissor-lift that cost him his Hawthorn vice-captaincy.

item.title: Why financial markets have thrown logic to the wind
item.description: "Up the stairs and down the elevator" has been the rule of thumb for decades in financial markets — but apparently it's not the case any longer.

item.title: ACCC to provide weekly fuel updates amid soaring prices driven by Middle East war
item.description: Australia's consumer watchdog is watching fuel companies as prices surge past $2.50 a litre, driven by a Middle East war that has pushed global oil to nearly $US120 a barrel.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Philippines celebrate as jaw-dropping free kick lights up final group games
item.description: India's Manisha Kaylan channels Cristiano Ronaldo to score the goal of the tournament as the Philippines progress despite not playing since Sunday. Here's all you may have missed from the final night of group stage action at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Patient detained under mental health laws waited more than four days in emergency department for bed
item.description: An alleged murder by a man who absconded from Cumberland Hospital shines a spotlight on a NSW mental healthcare system that may be more broken than first thought.

item.title: Gari Yala report finds racism against First Nations workers widespread
item.description: A landmark Indigenous-led report reveals that racism remains a daily reality for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander workers and, at the current rate of change, "it will take 118 years to eliminate racial slurs from Australian workplaces".

item.title: Will MotoGP in the Adelaide parklands reignite the land grab debate this election?
item.description: With the announcements of MotoGP and LIV Golf taking up public green spaces, environment has become a key concern raised by South Australian voters to ABC's Your Say.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 11: Can you find your way around the world?
item.description: Where would you find the Frank Lloyd Wright marvel, Fallingwater? And what's the capital of Fiji?

item.title: Cannot find Greek yoghurt in the supermarket aisle? You are not alone
item.description: Greek yoghurt has been flying off the shelves as viral social media trends spruik its high-protein benefits.

item.title: Nationals face leadership contest after David Littleproud steps down
item.description: Four Nationals are emerging as contenders to lead the party, with Bridget McKenzie, Matt Canavan and Kevin Hogan declaring their candidacy.

item.title: Bundaberg flooding
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Search for two international tourists missing in widespread flooding
item.description: A car belonging to two international tourists who failed to arrive at their destination has been found.

item.title: Foreign Facebook accounts using AI Pauline Hanson to manipulate Australians
item.description: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation uncovers a raft of foreign Facebook accounts targeting Australian users. And they're just the tip of the iceberg.

item.title: Australia closes several Middle East diplomatic missions, Wong confirms — as it happened
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has confirmed Australia has closed several diplomatic missions in the Middle East amid the ongoing war.

item.title: Women with Asherman's syndrome call for tighter control of outdated practices
item.description: Outdated medical devices are being used to perform a common obstetrics procedure on Australian women that can cause a condition known as Asherman's syndrome.

item.title: Bundaberg residents told not to return home yet after flood watch downgraded - as it happened
item.description: Bundaberg is facing its worst flood in more than a decade, with the Burnett River peaking at about 7.4 metres.

item.title: Australian Medical Association speaks out after top doctor suspended from Albury Wodonga Health
item.description: The Australian Medical Association is reviewing why several top doctors and nurses have left Albury Wodonga hospital.

item.title: South Australian Dan Turner completes dream of sailing around the world in homemade boat
item.description: After more than a year of sailing and thousands of nautical miles covered, Dan Turner completes a race around the globe in the boat he built in his driveway.

item.title: Woman who lost life savings in phishing scam takes on ING Bank, and wins
item.description: Claudia Lee's ING savings account was drained by a scammer while she was travelling overseas — but it took 18 months for the bank to be forced to take responsibility.

item.title: ASX posts modest win as Asian markets regain ground — as it happened
item.description: A positive day on the ASX 200 as Australia's market posts gains despite Westpac and the National Australia Bank predicting the RBA will lift rates by 25 basis points in March and May.

item.title: Family members of people killed in e-rideable accidents left disappointed by lack of urgency from authorities
item.description: The families of two West Australians killed in e-scooter accidents say they are disappointed the state government is not acting sooner to better regulate e-rideable devices.

item.title: UEFA Champions League: Spurs keeper hooked after 17 minutes against Atletico, Liverpool upset by Galatasaray, Yamal penalty denies Newcastle
item.description: Tottenham's nightmare season drops to an embarrassing new low as Antonin Kinsky is marched off the field by his manager in Madrid.

item.title: Matt Denny and Lawrence Okoye bring Ashes rivalry to discus, even as training partners
item.description: Having competed against each other with some of the biggest prizes in discus on the line, Matt Denny and Lawrence Okoye are now training together and pushing each other to new heights.

item.title: Mineral exploration at record highs in Western Australia's modern-day gold rush
item.description: The surge in Australian dollar gold prices in the past year is being reflected across regional Western Australia, where gold exploration has hit record levels.

item.title: Bundaberg Flooding drone footage
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Member of Iranian football team granted asylum requests return to Iran
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says one of the seven members of the Iranian women's football delegation granted asylum in Australia has changed their mind and wants to return to Iran. Iranian embassy officials have collected the woman from the hotel where she was under police protection.

item.title: Australian designer Katie Perry wins trademark case against popstar Katy Perry in High Court
item.description: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry wins her High Court battle against international pop star Katy Perry in a long-running trademark case.

item.title: NACC Robodebt investigation finds two people engaged in serious corrupt conduct
item.description: Two people investigated over the so-called "Robodebt" scheme have been found to have engaged in serious corrupt conduct, while four others, including former prime minister Scott Morrison, have been cleared.

item.title: Indigenous Dance College NAISDA celebrates 50 years of cultural pride
item.description: Australia's longest-running First Nations dance academy has helped to shape the cultural fabric of the nation.

item.title: There is a constant reminder in Roebourne of a massacre 160 years ago
item.description: In the Pilbara town of Roebourne the name of many streets haunt residents as they are named after perpetrators of a massacre which decimated the Yaburara tribe 160 years ago.

item.title: Police return to Oak Park station to search for evidence in case of missing boy Gus Lamont
item.description: SA Police have returned to the family's remote outback station as they continue their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old Gus Lamont.

item.title: Major floods threaten NT remote communities of Daly River and Beswick, as return to school begins in Katherine
item.description: Schools around Katherine are starting to reopen in the wake of flooding over the weekend, though major flooding is continuing to threaten a remote Northern Territory community nearby.

item.title: Drone footage of Chinchilla on Wednesday morning
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA Liberal Party candidate says 'same-sex marriage is not real' and 'feminism is demonic'
item.description: A Liberal candidate says "same-sex marriage is not real" and "feminism is demonic", in views Liberal leader Ashton Hurn says she does not share, but will leave up to voters to decide. Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

item.title: Video taken this morning in Bundaberg shows floodwaters surging through the city.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Indian Wells 2026: Talia Gibson scores upset win over Jasmine Paolini, Aryna Sabalenka outduels Naomi Osaka
item.description: Talia Gibson scores one of the biggest wins of her young career as she advances at Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka shows off one of the newest members of her team.

item.title: Queen Camilla delivers message of support to survivors of all types of violence
item.description: Queen Camilla has delivered a speech marking International Women's Day with a message to survivors of all types of violence, saying: "Please know that you are not alone."

item.title: Matt Canavan vows to 'fight back' as he rises to the Nationals leadership
item.description: With a populist economic message, the new Nationals leader said local manufacturing and higher birth rates were key as he rejected the labels of "left" and "right" politics.

item.title: St Basil's aged care home in Fawkner fined $150k over breaches relating to fatal COVID outbreak
item.description: St Basil's aged care in Fawkner has been fined $150,000 for failing to provide adequate training to staff in 2020.

item.title: Brisbane Roar offers training and support to Iranian asylum seeker players
item.description: A-League clubs reach out to the Iranian women's players granted asylum, offering training, support and a new "home".

item.title: UN inquiry finds Russia's deportations of 20,000 Ukrainian children amounts to crimes against humanity
item.description: A UN investigation has found Russia's deportation and transfer of Ukrainian children since Moscow's full-scale invasion in 2022 amounts to crimes against humanity.

item.title: Australia's pesticide regulator sets new rules banning some rat baits from public sale
item.description: Rat baits could soon be restricted to licensed operators after a change in regulations from the Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority.

item.title: Doctor expresses concern for memory of driver who crashed into group of people in Daylesford
item.description: The diabetes doctor of the driver responsible for a 2023 collision in the Victorian town of Daylesford, that killed five people, tells a coronial inquest that he is "concerned" for his patient's memory.

item.title: Why Iran is targeting UAE more than other Gulf states in war with US and Israel
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is reporting more Iranian attacks than any other country as Tehran continues to retaliate against US and Israeli strikes.

item.title: Corio man charged over triple-fatal crash on Western Highway at Armstrong near Ararat
item.description: Two Victorians and a South Australian man died in the crash involving five vehicles on the Western Highway near Ararat.

item.title: Tony Popovic turns attention to finer details as Socceroos close in on 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic is known for his attention to detail and is leaving no stone unturned as he prepares his squad for the World Cup.

item.title: Farmers devastated by NT floods feel overlooked by emergency response
item.description: NT farmers counting the cost of livestock deaths and crop damage say they feel overlooked by the emergency flood response.

item.title: Adam Marshall MP celebrates official opening of NSW DPI HQ in 2020
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WOMADelaide 2026 review: How a city found joy in a global music celebration
item.description: At Adelaide's global music weekender, tens of thousands transform everyday anxieties into a shared burst of joy.

item.title: Anonymous gamblers betting on Iran strikes make millions on Polymarket
item.description: Gambling experts and US lawmakers have raised concerns about the ethics of online betting and whether insider information is helping profiteers cash in on war.

item.title: Darwin River residents say they have 'lost everything' in flooding that struck without warning
item.description: People have had to be rescued from croc-inhabited waters and some have lost everything following flooding in Darwin River.

item.title: Bam Adebayo's 83 points for Miami Heat move him past Kobe Bryant on NBA's all-time single-game scoring chart
item.description: Bam Adebayo of the Miami Heat scores 83 points in a game against the Washington Wizards, placing him above Kobe Bryant in the NBA's all-time single-game scoring charts.

item.title: Perth Festival 2026: Our favourites from Haribo Kimchi to Ngaiire
item.description: Operatic punk, a Korean snack stand on stage, and a return to the golden age of dance halls were some of the highlights of the festival in 2026.

item.title: Fuel shortages hit rural Queensland businesses
item.description: Growing fuel uncertainty is hitting rural and fisheries businesses across regional Queensland, potentially costing them thousands in lost production and higher operating costs.

item.title: Linq Buslines and two directors plead guilty to safety breaches after deadly crash
item.description: The bus company linked to the deadly 2023 Hunter Valley wedding bus crash and a director plead guilty to heavy-vehicle law safety breaches.

item.title: Government doubles graduate visa fee but Pacific Island countries exempt
item.description: Tiered pricing for visas is being used to support Pacific nations but some fear it could be misused to target certain communities.

item.title: Sarah Game tells voters not to put her party first after split with Chris McDermott
item.description: A South Australian politician is urging voters to not back her own party in the upper house after a dramatic breakdown with star candidate and former Adelaide Crows captain Chris McDermott.

item.title: Doongmabulla Springs case proceeds to civil trial in Queensland's Supreme Court
item.description: The dispute centres on the Doongmbulla Springs, near the Carmichael River in central Queensland, with traditional custodians accusing the Queensland government of failing to protect the sacred site.

item.title: Boil water alert remains for Darwin area after flood takes out dam pump
item.description: People in Darwin, Palmerstorn and its surrounding areas will need to keep boiling water before consumption for the foreseeable future, as work to restore the Darwin River Dam's pumps continue.

item.title: Nicholas James Alexander jailed over antisemitic attacks across Sydney
item.description: Nicholas James Alexander was jailed for a maximum of five years for his role orchestrating a series of antisemitic attacks across Sydney, including the fire-bombing of a childcare centre.

item.title: Cracks shut Great Western Highway's Convict Bridge for at least two weeks
item.description: A crucial bridge on the Great Western Highway linking Sydney to the state's Central West will be shut for at least a fortnight while specialists assess why it is moving and cracking.

item.title: Matt Canavan is National Party's unusual leader with dreams of fossil-fuel powered future
item.description: Matt Canavan once claimed to be an atypical Nationals member. Now the pro-mining Queenslander is leading the party from the Senate.

item.title: IDF build up on Israel-Lebanon border
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aerial footage of Darwin River floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Assessing the flood damage at Darwin River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women tell court Barry Cable sexually assaulted them as children in Melbourne backyard spas
item.description: Former footballer Barry Cable sexually assaulted children in a backyard spa in Melbourne at a time when he was a "famous" figure, a court has heard.

item.title: The Tasmania Devils won't play AFL at Macquarie Point earlier than 2031. Should we be surprised?
item.description: Tasmania's future AFL club believed they would be playing in their new $1.13-billion stadium from 2030, but it's now clear their debut there will be at least one year later.

item.title: Economists scramble to change their interest rate forecasts after RBA speaks
item.description: Expectations the Reserve Bank will hike the cash rate next week are rising as leading economists shift their forecasts — and some say one increase may not be enough.

item.title: Mother of Iranian women's footballer pleads with player to stay in Australia as families face threats
item.description: The smiles and joy seen in pictures of the seven members of the Iranian women's football team who sought asylum in Australia only tell part of the story, with many still scared for their families back home.

item.title: More views of SA Liberal candidate emerge saying Islam 'poisonous, wrong', 'trans agenda is insanity'
item.description: More views by Liberal Party candidate and evangelical Christian Carston Woodhouse emerge with the Adelaide man saying Islam is "poisonous and wrong" and the "trans agenda is insanity". Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

item.title: Flower importers feel the pinch as Iran war affects flight routes
item.description: The war in the Middle East is not just affecting petrol prices and travel plans — florists and their suppliers have suddenly found themselves unable to supply blooms due to the disruption of air freight routes.

item.title: CLP knocks back bid to refer Tanzil Rahman bullying claims to parliamentary inquiry
item.description: A Northern Territory government backbencher accused of bullying has faced a bid for a parliamentary inquiry into his alleged conduct, as well as an extraordinary berating from the NT Speaker.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: WA government rules out petrol price cap as fuel costs soar amid Middle East war
item.description: The WA government has moved to allay concerns around fuel price and supply, after a roundtable meeting in Perth on Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Teenage pair rack up charges during alleged cross-state crime spree
item.description: A Mildura man has been remanded in custody and his accomplice remains on the run after the pair allegedly went on a four-day crime spree across Victoria.

item.title: Tasmania Police officer and a Franklin River blockade photographer reunite after four decades
item.description: Ian Wright captured the Franklin River blockade on film. Forty years later, the officer who arrested him reached out.

item.title: Magnitude-4.4 earthquake detected in Boorowa, NSW
item.description: Thousands of people report feeling a magnitude-4.4 earthquake that has hit Boorowa in southern New South Wales.

item.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
item.description: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

item.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

item.title: Matt Canavan may blunt One Nation but can he help rebuild the Coalition?
item.description: Matt Canavan's rise to the Nationals leadership gives the Coalition a fighter capable of taking on Pauline Hanson, but beating back One Nation is only part of the opposition's electoral dilemma.

item.title: Victorian government vows to shift building inspection costs from buyers to sellers
item.description: The Victorian government says it will make home sellers pay for building and pest inspections rather than home buyers if it wins this year's state election in November.

item.title: Teachers, schools given handbook to combat misogynistic behaviour in classrooms
item.description: Teachers say misogynistic behaviour from young, male students has been getting worse. Now there's a guide to help.

item.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab
item.description: Protests erupted following the killing of six-year-old Hind Rajab at the hands of Israeli forces. Two years later, her story is in the running for an Academy Award.

item.title: Victorian wineries sell vineyards to gold miners amid global headwinds
item.description: As the wine industry struggles with increased challenges, some Victorian wineries are choosing to sell their land to mining companies.

item.title: Approval granted for major expansion of Porters Bridge Road Quarry despite concerns
item.description: Locals say the "continuous rumbling" from a quarry in Tasmania's Meander Valley is a major disturbance. The company which owns it has just been given approval to increase production by 500 per cent.

item.title: Countries agree to 'largest ever' oil stocks release as more ships hit in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The International Energy Agency agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such move in its history.

item.title: Kharg Island could be Iran's weak spot as Washington reportedly considers ground invasion options
item.description: There is a small island in the Persian Gulf that is vital to the flow of Iranian oil to its customers and, analysts say, if Washington or Israel were to seize it the regime's war chest would soon dry up.

item.title: Iconic Australian-made pinball machines preserved for generations to come
item.description: Five pinball machines, including the first licensed Star Wars unit, have been saved from the scrap heap and will eventually be made available for the public to play at a special exhibition.

item.title: Live: Trump says he is not worried about Iran-backed attack in US
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation reportedly warns police departments in California that Iran could retaliate to US attacks by launching drones at the US West Coast. Follow live.

item.title: Lauren Parker finishes eighth in women's 10km sitting para cross-country skiing, Oksana Masters wins gold
item.description: Lauren Parker has finished eighth in the women's 10km sitting para cross-country skiing at the Milano Cortina Games, just nine months after taking up the sport.

item.title: Kalyn Ponga makes international rugby league switch to represent New Zealand
item.description: Kalyn Ponga says previous appearances for Australia did not reflect his true national identity after the International Rugby League approved his application to switch allegiances to New Zealand.

item.title: Korean-born Australian woman overturns adoption as federal inquiry launched
item.description: A Korean-born Australian who was sexually assaulted growing up is calling for changes to adoption laws after struggling to remove her adoptive parents from her birth certificate.

item.title: Alleged carjacker arested and charged over Seabrook crash
item.description: A 19-year-old man will face court after walking away from a crash which tore a carjacked vehicle in half in Melbourne's south-west last week.

item.title: Adelaide Hills residents living near fertiliser firm Neutrog want answers on air quality
item.description: Residents in the Adelaide Hills town of Kanmantoo and surrounds have been dealing with a growing regularity of pungent odour but the fertiliser company, who pleaded guilty to environmental breaches, says its chicken litter presents no risk.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: ABC Oscars quiz: How well do you remember the winners and losers from the past 10 years?
item.description: Test your knowledge of who went home with a statue and who was honoured just to be nominated.

item.title: Developers mainly building 'luxury' apartments in Brisbane despite affordable housing demand
item.description: Developers and economists say the apartment mix — or lack of — is the result of increasing building and labour costs, competition for labour, growing demand from owner-occupiers and the need for private developers to stay profitable.

item.title: German Paralympic Games protest of Russian gold medallist under investigation
item.description: Germany's Paralympians stage their own protest against Russia's inclusion at the Milano Cortina Games, while Ukraine says its athletes have been mistreated by the International Paralympic ‌Committee.

item.title: Sydney's abnormal heat, humidity levels due to warm Tasman Sea and northerly winds
item.description: Sydney's humidity has been rivalling the tropics in the middle of wet season, with the stifling conditions expected to linger into autumn.

item.title: Evacuation warning issued as Chinchilla prepares for flood peak and Bundaberg counts cost
item.description: Residents in Chinchilla and parts of central Queensland brace for the worst as floodwater continues to rise, despite conditions easing towards the coast.

item.title: Police return to Oak Park Station in search for Gus Lamont
item.description: SA Police officers continue their resumed search for missing four-year-old Gus Lamont after returning to Oak Park Station in Yunta.

item.title: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking announces shock exit
item.description: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking resigns amid rumours he is seeking political pre-selection.

item.title: Live: Australia considering request to release oil reserves amid Middle East war
item.description: Australia is finalising its response to a request for the release of oil reserves amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Follow live.

item.title: UK prime minister warned of 'reputational risk' over ex-ambassador's links to Epstein, new files show
item.description: Fresh questions are being asked about the British prime minister's judgement in appointing Peter Mandelson as the UK's ambassador to the US.

item.title: Shoalhaven residents to choose between military helicopters to replace 'chopper on a stick' landmark
item.description: Residents on the NSW South Coast are being asked to decide which model helicopter they want to replace Nowra's "chopper on a stick" landmark.

item.title: North Queensland residents say electoral boundary changes leave them 'short-changed'
item.description: With one parliamentarian from the far north set to go, residents in some areas will go from being 15 minutes' drive away from their local MP to 15 hours.

item.title: More than 100 doctors file no-confidence vote in regional health service executive management
item.description: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

item.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
item.description: Some advocates say vitamin IV drips can promote clearer thinking, immunity, beauty and even anti-aging, but health experts say there is little research to back the claims.

item.title: Iran in doubt to play men's FIFA World Cup following US air strikes
item.description: Iran's sports minister says his country cannot compete in the men's FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by the United States.

item.title: Live: ASX to fall, following drop on Wall St
item.description: The ASX 200 is set to drop, following a negative afternoon on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
